transcript.text: Hello, hello. This is a request video. What do I think about politics? Like what political party I am? I'm not in a political party. When I was voting last, and I had the decision, you know, I only had two decisions to vote for between Democrat or Republican.

I was always choosing Democrats. I think Democrats are a lot better than Republicans. They're more in line with me, with social issues. They're more, you know, freedom-based and open-minded. I'm not trying to control you with making, you know, abortion illegal and all that, making marijuana illegal and all that kind of stuff.
They're also, I think, less beholden to the corporations.
For example, if you look at TV, Fox News is all about the Republicans and against the Democrats. And then I guess NBC would be more Democrat and more against Republican.

But, I mean, just Fox, they just give me the creeps. I don't watch them that much. I was watching them a little bit back when I was living in my van because I was in the gym walking on the treadmill and watching them a little bit.
And, like, every single day, they just have to have another problem with Obama. Like, that's all they care about.
And they use everything. They use so much that it becomes just abundantly obvious to me that that's all they care about. You know, they'll use anything.
And they also lie. The Republicans, they lie through their teeth in Fox News. It's unbelievable.

Like, Paul Ryan, when they were doing the debate, Paul Ryan was saying that about some, like, coal factory or something in his hometown that was, through some over-regulation, was closed down by Obama.
But it was closed down in 2008 before Obama became president. And, yeah, his tax plan was all skewed and for, you know, for rich people.
And even the way that that guy talks, Paul Ryan, he's just, like, with that sneer, that angry sneer.
And then Bill O'Reilly, how he talks with, like, a gangster, basically.
Hey, what's up? You know, with the people, come on. You know, he's, like, he's such a thug. He's so, like, low class.
And, I mean, and he says stuff that doesn't make any sense. Like, he said, he said they were talking about a strip club that opened up next to a nunnery and now a convict.

He said that the nuns' human rights were being violated because the strip club wanted to open up next to him, which doesn't make any sense because, you know, the women are clothed when they walk in there.
It's the strip club's rights that are being violated. But he makes stuff up, too.
Like, when he talked about Jesse Ventura saying that, like, that guy, Chris Kyle, you know, interviewed him on his show.
And he said he punched him and knocked him down. And Jesse Ventura said, I don't know what you're talking about. I've never met the guy.

And then, because, like, supposedly Jesse Ventura was saying that, like, saying that he was glad that his friends were killed in Afghanistan because they found out that Jesse Ventura was against the war in Iraq.
And so then they come back and Bill O'Reilly has two ladies on his show saying that, oh, we have sworn affidavits from two people saying that they witnessed it.
But they didn't even bring the people on or film them saying anything. And, like, why would you even go out of your way to say that?
Like, that's not even news. That's just propaganda.
So they just try to turn people against the, you know, anybody who says anything negative against them.
They go after them. And it's just ridiculous.
The Benghazi thing, for example. Benghazi, you know, the American embassy in Libya, where that was attacked by all those terrorists.
And they killed the ambassador and, like, four guards, you know.
And supposedly they said, oh, we need backup. And Hillary Clinton got on the phone and said, oh, you know, stand down or something.
And then when they talked about it, they didn't say that it was a terrorist group because they didn't even know who it was, you know.
They didn't want to just say it was. So then Fox News gets on there and says, oh, my God, yeah, you know, it's Obama's fault.
Why did they say stand down? Why did they say stand down? And then in the talking points, why did they say it wasn't a terrorist group?
What did they do? You know, and then they use that to try to say that he needs to be impeached.
You know, like Obama needs to be impeached because his secretary of state, like, said stand down when they should have gone in there.
And, like, you know, I mean, a simple mistake like that and you need to impeach the president, it's just so, like, they'll just choose anything.
And they'll say and they have this tone to them of just, you know, like Glenn Beck and these guys are just like they're taking our freedoms.
We have to take to power back. Oh, my God.
They're, you know, they're such, they're such criminals.
We are the world's going to collapse if we if we let them take over, you know, and they use all this like they get people riled up and and.
And it works, though, you know, they use all these fear tactics and like.
Kind of like Hitler, remember how Hitler used to give those speeches where he's all riled up and just playing foaming at the mouth and.
Oh, my God, the Jews, we have to do something or we're going to lose, you know, and then that that works.
It works to get people behind you.
